what: One-off solver-collapse diagnostic behind findings 1/3 of docs/results/terwilliger-reach-probe-2026-07-01.md: distinguishes CLARABEL-only collapse (SCS recovers) from both-solver collapse (conditioning wall; d>=10 formulation faithfulness unvalidated) on anchor cells, plus a k_max bisect at the two both-solver-collapse cells. Measured 2026-07-01 on the operator machine (cvxpy 1.9.2, numpy 2.5.0) via terwilliger_sdp.solve_primal(n, d, k_max=..., solver=...).
reading: anchor = a value the true SDP optimum cannot be below (A(n,d) itself, or Schrijver's own Table I value computed from the SAME formulation). 'optimal' status with value far below the anchor = solver collapse or formulation over-constraint; the chaotic scatter across solvers and k_max (rather than two solvers agreeing on one wrong value) points to ill-conditioning (unnormalized beta blocks, Q-pit-2) as the first suspect, but a d>=10-specific transcription hole is NOT excluded by float evidence - deciding requires a fixed solve leg or an exact feasible-point check (see the follow-up in the results doc).
reproduce: for (n,d,km,solver) of interest: python3 -c "import importlib.util; spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('ts','scripts/terwilliger_sdp.py'); ts=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(ts); r=ts.solve_primal(N,D,k_max=KM,solver='SOLVER'); print(r['status'], r['value'])" (float values are solver-version-dependent; the PATTERN - which cells collapse under which solver - is the finding)
